approach: cull half only of reconcile-against-mux (issue #135): listPanes adapter primitive + mux-scoped reconcile(ctx) cull in identity.ts, wired behind who --reconcile and prune. Adopt (#136) and retention/GC (#137) explicitly out of scope, filed as separate follow-up issues, not this CR.
additions to unit/registry (not a new node) — reconcile extends prune's guard set and who's listing, same file/concern

why: Cold sdd-spec-judge 3-lens over the added scenarios: oracle/builder/architect all PASS, ALIGNED, no blocker. Additive-only edit to registry.feature (9 added / 0 modified / 0 removed, confirmed via gherkin-cli diff) on an already-@frozen suite — self-clears, no re-open. One advisory content gap (missing herdr-side positive-cull parity scenario) resolved before this gate by adding it. README prose (bullets, non-goals, mapping table) consistent 1:1 with the 9 new scenarios.

Cold sdd-impl-judge re-derived each of the 9 frozen scenarios independently: 9/9 PASS. listPanes on SessionAdapter (herdr JSON pane list dropping agentless scaffold panes; tmux list-panes -a -F id+cmd+cwd, no harness); reconcile(ctx) mux-scoped cull (currentPane-gated, pane.mux===cur.mux, standing/pane-null exempt); prune seeds from reconcile then falls through to its existing per-record paneExists+staleness loop (confirmed no double-counting); who --reconcile / top-level who --reconcile wired mirroring --all. pnpm typecheck clean, pnpm test 326/326 green (incl. 9 new reconcile/listPanes tests + fixed ambient-env leakage in 8 pre-existing prune test fixtures that omitted env, exposed by reconcile's new env-sensitivity). Structural read: diff scoped to session.ts/session.tmux.ts/session.herdr.ts/identity.ts/cli.ts + tests; grepped for #136 adopt and #137 retention/GC logic, none found. Non-blocking observation: reconcile's kind==='standing' guard is currently dead code (standing records already excluded by the pane-null check) — harmless defense-in-depth, left as-is for symmetry with prune's loop shape.
